stimulus: Most attorneys at the firm specialize in corporate law. Most attorneys at the firm also hold an advanced certification in tax law. No attorney without an advanced certification in tax law is assigned to the mergers division.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Most attorneys at the firm are assigned to the mergers division.
choice_b: All attorneys in the mergers division specialize in corporate law.
choice_c: No attorney who specializes in corporate law lacks a tax law certification.
choice_d: Some attorneys who specialize in corporate law hold an advanced certification in tax law.
choice_e: The mergers division includes only attorneys who specialize in corporate law.
explanation: Most attorneys specialize in corporate law and most hold the tax certification. Since both groups constitute a majority of the same pool, they must overlap. Therefore some corporate law specialists must also hold the tax certification.
explanation_a: Incorrect. While most hold the certification needed for the mergers division, the stimulus does not say that all certified attorneys are assigned there.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus does not link the mergers division to corporate law specialization; it links it to tax certification.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Most, not all, attorneys have each attribute. Some corporate law specialists could lack the certification.
explanation_d: Correct. When two majorities draw from the same group, they necessarily overlap. So some corporate law specialists must also be tax-certified.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The mergers division requires tax certification, but the stimulus does not require corporate law specialization for that division.

stimulus: All migratory birds in the northern corridor stop at Lake Harmon during autumn. Most birds that stop at Lake Harmon feed on the lake's native insects. The population of native insects at Lake Harmon has declined sharply this year due to pesticide runoff.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: All migratory birds in the northern corridor will be affected by the decline in native insects.
choice_b: Some migratory birds in the northern corridor feed on Lake Harmon's native insects.
choice_c: The migratory bird population in the northern corridor will decline this year.
choice_d: Most migratory birds in the northern corridor feed on native insects at Lake Harmon.
choice_e: Pesticide runoff will cause migratory birds to avoid Lake Harmon.
explanation: All northern corridor migratory birds stop at Lake Harmon. Most birds that stop there feed on native insects. So most of the migratory birds feed on the insects, which means at least some do.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Only most, not all, birds that stop at the lake feed on the insects. Some may not be affected.
explanation_b: Correct. All corridor migratory birds stop at the lake, and most lake-stoppers feed on native insects. So at least some migratory birds feed on those insects.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The insect decline may affect food supply, but the stimulus does not guarantee a population decline among the birds.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Most birds stopping at the lake feed on insects, and all migratory birds stop there, so most migratory birds likely feed on them — but 'most of those who stop' does not strictly guarantee 'most migratory birds' without knowing the composition.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no basis for concluding that birds will change their migratory behavior.

stimulus: Every student enrolled in the honors program takes at least one philosophy course. Some students enrolled in the honors program are also members of the debate team. All members of the debate team participate in the annual tournament.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Some students who take philosophy courses participate in the annual tournament.
choice_b: All members of the debate team take at least one philosophy course.
choice_c: Every student in the honors program participates in the annual tournament.
choice_d: Most members of the debate team are enrolled in the honors program.
choice_e: The annual tournament includes only honors program students.
explanation: Some honors students are on the debate team. All honors students take philosophy. All debate team members participate in the tournament. So those students who are in both honors and debate take philosophy AND participate in the tournament.
explanation_a: Correct. Some honors students are on the debate team. Those students take philosophy (honors requirement) and participate in the tournament (debate requirement). So some philosophy students participate in the tournament.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Only some debate team members are in the honors program. Debate team members not in honors may not take philosophy.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Only honors students who are also on the debate team must participate. Honors students not on the team have no such requirement.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says some honors students are on the debate team, not that most debaters are in honors.
explanation_e: Incorrect. All debate team members participate, but non-honors debaters may also be in the tournament.

stimulus: No genuine Renaissance fresco uses synthetic pigments, since synthetic pigments were not developed until the nineteenth century. Several paintings recently attributed to Bellini contain traces of synthetic pigments. Art historians consider Bellini a master of the Renaissance fresco tradition.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: Art historians have incorrectly attributed the paintings to Bellini.
choice_b: Synthetic pigments are inferior to the pigments used in Renaissance frescoes.
choice_c: The paintings recently attributed to Bellini that contain synthetic pigments are not genuine Renaissance frescoes.
choice_d: Bellini never used synthetic pigments in his work.
choice_e: Bellini occasionally experimented with materials outside the Renaissance tradition.
explanation: No genuine Renaissance fresco uses synthetic pigments. The paintings in question contain synthetic pigments. Therefore those specific paintings cannot be genuine Renaissance frescoes.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The paintings might not be genuine Renaissance frescoes but could still have been painted by Bellini at a different time or in a different style.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus makes no qualitative comparison between synthetic and natural pigments.
explanation_c: Correct. If no genuine Renaissance fresco contains synthetic pigments, then any painting with synthetic pigments is not a genuine Renaissance fresco, regardless of who painted it.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says Bellini was a Renaissance fresco master, but the paintings with synthetic pigments might be misattributed rather than proving Bellini never used them.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus does not establish that Bellini himself used synthetic pigments; the paintings may be misattributed.

stimulus: If a witness refuses to testify, the court may hold the witness in contempt. Any witness held in contempt faces a fine or imprisonment. Henderson, a key witness in the fraud case, has refused to testify despite being subpoenaed.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Henderson will be fined or imprisoned.
choice_b: The court will hold Henderson in contempt.
choice_c: The subpoena was lawfully issued.
choice_d: Henderson's refusal will weaken the prosecution's case.
choice_e: Henderson may face a fine or imprisonment.
explanation: Henderson refused to testify, so the court may hold Henderson in contempt. If held in contempt, Henderson faces a fine or imprisonment. Therefore Henderson may face a fine or imprisonment.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The court may hold Henderson in contempt, not that it will. So the fine or imprisonment is possible but not certain.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says the court may hold the witness in contempt, not that it must or will.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus mentions the subpoena but provides no information about the validity of its issuance.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address the effect of Henderson's refusal on the prosecution's case.
explanation_e: Correct. Since the court may hold Henderson in contempt, and contempt leads to a fine or imprisonment, Henderson may face those consequences.

stimulus: Any element with an atomic number greater than 83 is radioactive. Bismuth has an atomic number of 83. All radioactive elements decay over time, eventually producing stable isotopes. Polonium has an atomic number of 84.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Bismuth is not radioactive.
choice_b: All elements with atomic numbers less than 84 are stable.
choice_c: Polonium is more dangerous than bismuth.
choice_d: Polonium will eventually produce stable isotopes.
choice_e: Bismuth does not decay over time.
explanation: Polonium has atomic number 84, which is greater than 83, so it is radioactive. All radioactive elements decay and eventually produce stable isotopes. Therefore polonium will eventually produce stable isotopes.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus says elements with atomic number greater than 83 are radioactive. Bismuth is exactly 83, so the rule does not apply. But this does not prove bismuth is not radioactive.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus only says elements above 83 are radioactive. It does not state that those at or below 83 are stable.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about the relative danger of elements.
explanation_d: Correct. Polonium's atomic number of 84 exceeds 83, making it radioactive. Radioactive elements decay to produce stable isotopes.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address bismuth's decay properties; having atomic number 83 means the greater-than-83 rule does not apply, but other factors could apply.

stimulus: Most residents of Oakdale who voted in the last election support the new park proposal. Most residents of Oakdale who voted in the last election also oppose the proposed tax increase. The park proposal can only be funded through the proposed tax increase.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Some residents of Oakdale who support the park proposal also oppose the tax increase that would fund it.
choice_b: The tax increase will not pass.
choice_c: Most residents of Oakdale are inconsistent in their policy preferences.
choice_d: The park proposal will not receive enough votes to pass.
choice_e: Residents who oppose the tax increase do not understand its connection to the park proposal.
explanation: Most voters support the park and most voters oppose the tax increase. Since both groups are majorities of the same pool of voters, they must overlap. So some people support the park while opposing the very tax that funds it.
explanation_a: Correct. Two overlapping majorities from the same group must share members. Some voters both support the park and oppose the tax increase.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus describes current preferences but does not guarantee any particular electoral outcome.
explanation_c: Incorrect. While some hold seemingly conflicting views, the stimulus does not establish that most residents are inconsistent overall.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus describes current preferences but does not determine election outcomes.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about whether residents understand the connection between the two proposals.

stimulus: Every film in the retrospective was directed by someone who studied at the National Film Academy. Some films in the retrospective were shot entirely on location in Prague. No film shot entirely on location in Prague was completed in under six months.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: All films directed by National Film Academy graduates were in the retrospective.
choice_b: Every film shot in Prague was directed by a National Film Academy graduate.
choice_c: No film in the retrospective was completed in under six months.
choice_d: The National Film Academy encourages filming on location in Prague.
choice_e: Some films directed by National Film Academy graduates took at least six months to complete.
explanation: Some retrospective films were shot in Prague. Prague films took at least six months. All retrospective films were directed by Academy graduates. So those Prague-shot retrospective films were directed by Academy graduates and took at least six months.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus says all retrospective films were by Academy graduates, not that all Academy graduate films were in the retrospective.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus only discusses films in the retrospective that were shot in Prague, not all Prague-shot films generally.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Only the Prague-filmed entries took at least six months. Other retrospective films may have been completed more quickly.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about the Academy's filming preferences or recommendations.
explanation_e: Correct. Some retrospective films were shot in Prague and thus took at least six months. All retrospective films were directed by Academy graduates. So some Academy graduate films took at least six months.

stimulus: If a municipality adopts a rent control ordinance, landlords in that municipality cannot raise rent by more than three percent annually. Cedarville has adopted a rent control ordinance. If landlords cannot raise rent by more than three percent annually, some will defer building maintenance to reduce costs.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Rent control ordinances always lead to deteriorating buildings.
choice_b: Some landlords in Cedarville will defer building maintenance to reduce costs.
choice_c: All landlords in Cedarville oppose the rent control ordinance.
choice_d: Cedarville's tenants will benefit from the rent control ordinance.
choice_e: Landlords in Cedarville will defer building maintenance.
explanation: Cedarville adopted rent control, so landlords cannot raise rent more than three percent. When landlords face that limit, some defer maintenance. Therefore some Cedarville landlords will defer maintenance.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus says some landlords will defer maintenance, not that buildings will always deteriorate as a result of rent control generally.
explanation_b: Correct. The rent cap applies in Cedarville, and the stimulus states that under such a cap, some landlords defer maintenance. So some Cedarville landlords will do so.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about landlords' attitudes toward the ordinance.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address whether tenants will benefit overall; deferred maintenance could harm them.
explanation_e: Incorrect. This is too strong. The stimulus says some landlords will defer maintenance, not all.

stimulus: All proteins are composed of amino acid chains. Some amino acid chains fold into stable three-dimensional structures without assistance. Any amino acid chain that does not fold into a stable structure without assistance requires a chaperone protein.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: All proteins require chaperone proteins.
choice_b: Some proteins fold into stable structures without assistance.
choice_c: Any protein that does not fold without assistance requires a chaperone protein.
choice_d: Every amino acid chain that requires a chaperone protein is a protein.
choice_e: Chaperone proteins are not composed of amino acid chains.
explanation: All proteins are amino acid chains. Amino acid chains that don't fold without assistance require chaperones. So any protein that doesn't fold without assistance, being an amino acid chain, requires a chaperone.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Some amino acid chains fold without assistance, so some proteins may not require chaperones.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Some amino acid chains fold without assistance, but we cannot be certain that those particular chains are proteins.
explanation_c: Correct. Proteins are amino acid chains. Amino acid chains that do not fold without help need chaperones. So proteins that do not fold without help need chaperones.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Non-protein amino acid chains could also require chaperone proteins.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Chaperone proteins are proteins and thus are composed of amino acid chains, per the first premise.

stimulus: Most voters under thirty in the district favor stricter environmental regulations. Most voters under thirty in the district also favor increased military spending. Stricter environmental regulations and increased military spending would both require raising the federal budget.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: Most voters under thirty favor raising the federal budget.
choice_b: Younger voters are less concerned with fiscal responsibility than older voters.
choice_c: Some voters under thirty favor both stricter environmental regulations and increased military spending.
choice_d: The federal budget will increase as a result of younger voters' preferences.
choice_e: All voters under thirty support policies that raise the federal budget.
explanation: Most under-thirty voters favor environmental regulations and most favor military spending. Two majorities of the same group must overlap, so some favor both.
explanation_a: Incorrect. While those in the overlap favor both policies that require budget increases, the stimulus does not directly state they favor raising the budget itself.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus does not compare younger voters to older voters on any dimension.
explanation_c: Correct. Since both groups constitute a majority of voters under thirty, they must overlap. Some voters under thirty must favor both policies.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Voter preferences do not necessarily determine policy outcomes.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Only most, not all, favor each policy. Some under-thirty voters may favor neither.

stimulus: No operatic aria composed before 1750 uses a full orchestral accompaniment, since full orchestral forces were not standard before that date. Several arias in the newly discovered Viennese manuscript use full orchestral accompaniment. The manuscript was initially dated to 1720.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: The Viennese manuscript was not actually composed in 1720.
choice_b: The arias with full orchestral accompaniment in the manuscript were not composed before 1750.
choice_c: The initial dating of the manuscript to 1720 is incorrect.
choice_d: Full orchestral accompaniment is superior to earlier forms of accompaniment.
choice_e: The manuscript contains arias by multiple composers from different periods.
explanation: No pre-1750 aria uses full orchestral accompaniment. The manuscript's arias use full orchestral accompaniment. By contrapositive, those arias were not composed before 1750.
explanation_a: Incorrect. A manuscript can contain works from various periods. The manuscript itself may date to 1720 even if some arias were added later.
explanation_b: Correct. By contrapositive: if an aria uses full orchestral accompaniment, it was not composed before 1750.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The manuscript's date and the composition dates of individual arias within it may differ.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus makes a historical observation, not a qualitative judgment about accompaniment styles.
explanation_e: Incorrect. While possible, the stimulus does not provide enough information to guarantee this conclusion.

stimulus: Any property that is rezoned for commercial use must undergo an environmental impact assessment. The environmental impact assessment takes a minimum of ninety days. The Henderson parcel was rezoned for commercial use on January 15th.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: The Henderson parcel will pass the environmental impact assessment.
choice_b: Commercial development of the Henderson parcel will begin after the assessment.
choice_c: The environmental impact assessment of the Henderson parcel will take exactly ninety days.
choice_d: The Henderson parcel must undergo an environmental impact assessment.
choice_e: The Henderson parcel cannot be developed for commercial use before mid-April.
explanation: The Henderson parcel was rezoned for commercial use. Any property rezoned for commercial use must undergo an environmental impact assessment. Therefore the Henderson parcel must undergo one.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus requires the assessment but does not guarantee it will be passed.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus does not specify that development is planned, only that the assessment is required.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says the assessment takes a minimum of ninety days, but it could take longer.
explanation_d: Correct. The parcel was rezoned for commercial use, triggering the mandatory environmental impact assessment.
explanation_e: Incorrect. While the assessment takes ninety days minimum, the stimulus does not specify when commercial use may begin relative to the assessment.

stimulus: Every coral reef in the tropical zone hosts at least one species of cleaner shrimp. Cleaner shrimp survive only in water temperatures between 24 and 30 degrees Celsius. The reef at Manta Point is in the tropical zone, and its water temperature has risen to 32 degrees Celsius this summer.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: The reef at Manta Point no longer hosts any cleaner shrimp.
choice_b: The reef at Manta Point will lose all its marine species.
choice_c: Cleaner shrimp can adapt to temperatures above 30 degrees Celsius.
choice_d: The water temperature at Manta Point will return to normal.
choice_e: Cleaner shrimp at Manta Point cannot currently survive.
explanation: Cleaner shrimp survive only between 24 and 30 degrees. Manta Point's water is 32 degrees, which is outside that range. So cleaner shrimp at Manta Point cannot currently survive.
explanation_a: Incorrect. We know the shrimp cannot survive at 32 degrees, but we cannot confirm whether any have already left or died.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The temperature constraint applies specifically to cleaner shrimp. Other species may tolerate higher temperatures.
explanation_c: Incorrect. This contradicts the stimulus, which states they survive only between 24 and 30 degrees.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about future temperature trends.
explanation_e: Correct. The water is 32 degrees, outside the 24-30 range cleaner shrimp require. So any cleaner shrimp at Manta Point cannot currently survive.

stimulus: Most professors in the sociology department publish at least one peer-reviewed article per year. Most professors in the sociology department also serve on at least one university committee. Professors who both publish annually and serve on committees are eligible for an early sabbatical.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Most professors in the sociology department are eligible for an early sabbatical.
choice_b: All professors who publish annually are eligible for an early sabbatical.
choice_c: Some professors in the sociology department are eligible for an early sabbatical.
choice_d: No professor who fails to publish is eligible for an early sabbatical.
choice_e: The early sabbatical program encourages professors to serve on committees.
explanation: Most publish and most serve on committees. These two majorities from the same department must overlap, so some professors do both. Those who do both are eligible for early sabbatical. So some are eligible.
explanation_a: Incorrect. While the overlapping majorities guarantee some are eligible, we cannot determine that most are.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Eligibility requires both publishing and committee service. Publishers who don't serve on committees wouldn't qualify.
explanation_c: Correct. Two majorities of the same group must overlap, so some professors both publish and serve on committees, making them eligible.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says those who do both are eligible, but does not state that publishing is strictly necessary.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus describes eligibility criteria but says nothing about the program's motivational effects.

stimulus: If a novel is selected for the Booker longlist, it was published in the Commonwealth within the preceding twelve months. Harrison's novel was not published in the Commonwealth. Every novel on the Booker longlist receives a significant increase in sales.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: Harrison's novel was not selected for the Booker longlist.
choice_b: Only novels published in the Commonwealth receive significant sales increases.
choice_c: Harrison should have published the novel in the Commonwealth.
choice_d: Harrison's novel will not receive a significant increase in sales.
choice_e: The Booker Prize favors Commonwealth authors over others.
explanation: Booker longlist selection requires Commonwealth publication. Harrison's novel was not published in the Commonwealth. By contrapositive, Harrison's novel was not selected for the longlist.
explanation_a: Correct. By contrapositive: if a novel is on the longlist, it was published in the Commonwealth. Harrison's was not, so it was not selected.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says longlisted novels get sales increases, not that only Commonwealth novels do.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus describes eligibility rules but makes no normative claim about what Harrison should have done.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Although the novel won't get a Booker-related sales boost, it could receive a sales increase for other reasons.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The publication requirement is a rule of the prize, not evidence of favoritism.

stimulus: All evidence obtained without a valid warrant is inadmissible in court under the exclusionary rule. The recordings made by Detective Park were obtained without a valid warrant. If evidence is inadmissible, the prosecution cannot use it to establish guilt at trial.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: The defendant in Detective Park's case will be acquitted.
choice_b: Detective Park violated the defendant's constitutional rights.
choice_c: All of the prosecution's evidence was obtained without a warrant.
choice_d: Detective Park will face disciplinary action.
choice_e: The prosecution cannot use Detective Park's recordings to establish guilt at trial.
explanation: The recordings were obtained without a valid warrant, so they are inadmissible. Inadmissible evidence cannot be used to establish guilt at trial. Therefore the prosecution cannot use the recordings for that purpose.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The prosecution may have other evidence besides the recordings.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus addresses the admissibility of evidence but does not explicitly address constitutional rights violations.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says only that the recordings lacked a warrant, not that all prosecution evidence did.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus addresses the admissibility of evidence, not any consequences for Detective Park.
explanation_e: Correct. No valid warrant means the recordings are inadmissible, and inadmissible evidence cannot be used to establish guilt.

stimulus: Any plant that produces flowers relies on pollination to reproduce. Some plants that rely on pollination to reproduce are pollinated by wind rather than insects. No plant pollinated by wind produces nectar, since nectar serves to attract insect pollinators.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: All flowering plants are pollinated by insects.
choice_b: Most plants that rely on pollination are pollinated by wind.
choice_c: No wind-pollinated plant produces flowers.
choice_d: Some plants that rely on pollination do not produce nectar.
choice_e: Plants that produce nectar are always pollinated by insects.
explanation: Some pollination-reliant plants are wind-pollinated. Wind-pollinated plants don't produce nectar. So some pollination-reliant plants don't produce nectar.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Some pollination-reliant plants are wind-pollinated, so some flowering plants could be wind-pollinated.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says some, not most, pollination-reliant plants are wind-pollinated.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus does not exclude wind-pollinated plants from producing flowers.
explanation_d: Correct. Some pollination-reliant plants are wind-pollinated, and wind-pollinated plants don't produce nectar. So some pollination-reliant plants lack nectar.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says wind-pollinated plants don't produce nectar, but doesn't guarantee that all nectar-producers are insect-pollinated.

stimulus: Most attendees at the technology conference are software engineers. Most attendees at the conference also hold graduate degrees. Every attendee who is both a software engineer and holds a graduate degree was offered a position by at least one recruiting firm at the event.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: At least one attendee was offered a position by a recruiting firm.
choice_b: Most attendees were offered positions by recruiting firms.
choice_c: All software engineers at the conference were offered positions.
choice_d: No attendee without a graduate degree was offered a position.
choice_e: Recruiting firms prefer candidates with graduate degrees.
explanation: Most attendees are software engineers and most have graduate degrees. Two overlapping majorities guarantee some attendees are both. Those who are both were offered positions. So at least one attendee was offered a position.
explanation_a: Correct. The two majorities must overlap, producing at least one person who is both a software engineer and holds a graduate degree. That person was offered a position.
explanation_b: Incorrect. While the overlap guarantees some were offered positions, we cannot determine whether the overlap constitutes a majority.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Software engineers who lack graduate degrees would not meet the dual criterion.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says the dual-qualified attendees were offered positions but does not say that no one else was.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus describes offers made to those with both qualifications but does not attribute preferences to the firms.

stimulus: All archaeological sites designated as World Heritage Sites receive annual preservation funding. Some World Heritage Sites are located in regions experiencing armed conflict. No site in a region experiencing armed conflict can be safely accessed by preservation teams.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: No World Heritage Site can be safely accessed by preservation teams.
choice_b: Some sites that receive annual preservation funding cannot be safely accessed by preservation teams.
choice_c: All archaeological sites in conflict regions are World Heritage Sites.
choice_d: Preservation funding is wasted on sites that cannot be accessed.
choice_e: Sites in conflict regions will lose their World Heritage designation.
explanation: All World Heritage Sites receive preservation funding. Some World Heritage Sites are in conflict regions. Sites in conflict regions cannot be safely accessed. So some funded sites cannot be accessed.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Only some World Heritage Sites are in conflict regions. Those not in conflict regions can presumably be accessed.
explanation_b: Correct. Some World Heritage Sites are in conflict regions and thus receive funding but cannot be safely accessed by preservation teams.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says some World Heritage Sites are in conflict regions, not that all conflict-region sites are World Heritage Sites.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no evaluative judgment about whether the funding is wasted.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address whether conflict affects World Heritage designation status.
